# `[api/run-id-dot-validation-mismatch]` — write-side allows `.` in a run id, read-side rejected it → "No runs yet"

**Symptom (s31, operator-hit).** The dashboard HomeView showed **"No runs yet"** even
after runs completed (one in DONE). `GET /runs` returned `[]`; `daemon.log` spammed
`skipping unreadable/invalid run manifest run-…-OVERVIEW.md-….json` for **every** run —
not just the "3 corrupt" ones the s30 handoff assumed (those weren't corrupt either).

**Cause — a write/read validator drift on the dot.** A run id is generated by
`slugifyIntent` (`orchestrator/capabilities.ts`), which **deliberately preserves `.`**
(regex `[^A-Za-z0-9._-]`) — so an intent mentioning a filename (`Create docs/OVERVIEW.md`)
yields `run-<ts>-…-overview.md-…`. The WRITE path re-validates with `isPathSafeId`
(`/^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$/ && !includes("..")` — allows `.`, blocks `..`/separators) and writes
the manifest. But the READ path — `isRunManifest` (the `GET /runs` listing) plus the
`/runs/:id`, `/runs/:id/usage`, `PATCH /runs/:id` routes — validated the run id with the
**stricter** `safeIdSegment` (`/^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$/`, **no dot**). So every filename-derived
run id failed read-validation → skipped in the listing and 400'd on open. Any intent that
mentions a file (the common case) produced an invisible run.

**Fix (`a1c81d2`).** Reuse the write-side `isPathSafeId` as the run-id validator on the
read side (`const safeRunId = isPathSafeId`); swap the run-id checks in `isRunManifest` +
the three run routes. Task/escalation ids keep the strict `safeIdSegment` (they are kebab,
never dotted). Still traversal-safe: `..` and every separator stay rejected (`../evil`,
`foo..bar`, `a%2Fb`, `..%2Fsentinel` all still fail). codex: CLEAN.

**Lesson.** When a value is produced by one allowlist and consumed by another, the two
**must be the same function**, not two hand-written regexes that drift. A stricter reader
silently drops legitimate data (here: an entire UI surface) with no error the operator can
act on — only a `WARN` buried in a log.
